per_standard_notes.no-inline-code: Not applicable — hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh is a .sh hook file, not a .md command/agent file. Standard 1 scopes to .md files in .claude/commands/ and .claude/agents/ only.
per_standard_notes.no-hardcoded-values: All runtime-variable values use env var overrides: CLAUDE_HOME (line 8), CLAUDE_TMPDIR (line 9), CLAUDE_PYTHON_BIN (line 10), CLAUDE_DAEMON_RESTART_GRANT_DIR (line 14), CLAUDE_DAEMON_RESTART_AUDIT_LOG (line 249), CLAUDE_HOOK_CONTEXT_MEM_KB (line 1095), CLAUDE_HOOK_CONTEXT_TIMEOUT (line 1096). URL strings like https://api.life-ai.app, localhost:3000, /usr/bin/happy, /root/happy/packages/ are used exclusively as security detection patterns — literals the hook grep-matches against to block dangerous commands, not configurable runtime endpoints. User-facing hint strings (lines 1225-1226, 1279, 1288) reference external script paths and dev URLs; these are fixed policy constants in the hook and are pre-existing (see codex finding 2 rationale below). No new violations in the diff.
per_standard_notes.use-source-venv: PYTHON_BIN resolves to ${CLAUDE_HOME}/venv/bin/python as first choice (line 10); python3 fallback is used only when the venv binary is not executable (lines 11-13). This is the correct venv-prefer pattern. No bare python3 invocation without venv resolution.
per_standard_notes.integer-step-numbering: Not applicable to a .sh file. Standard 4 scopes to .md files.
per_standard_notes.meaningful-naming: pretool-bash-safety.sh is a descriptive, non-generic name. No forbidden pattern match.
per_standard_notes.english-only: File contains no CJK characters (U+4E00-U+9FFF). Unconditional Standard 6 scan of all agent/command directories found only the five canonical mascot rank labels in agents/style-inspector.md:333, which are covered by the documented Cycle-2 exemption (spec-20260518-225715 §5.1). No new violations.
per_standard_notes.merge-similar-scripts: Only one changed file in --changed-files scope. No applicable comparison.
per_standard_notes.concise-documentation: Not applicable to a .sh hook file. Standard 8 scopes to .md command/agent files.
per_standard_notes.parameterized-scripts: All variable values are parameterized via env vars (see no-hardcoded-values note). No unparameterized threshold or path values.
per_standard_notes.dangling-references: Standard 10 as written scopes to: (1) script references in .md command/agent files, and (2) Python import statements. This changed file is a .sh hook. Within the .sh file, library imports via Python subprocess reference hooks/lib/allowlist.py and hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py — both verified to exist. The sentinel-writer reference scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py was also verified to exist. User-facing hint strings at lines 1225-1226 mention scripts/deploy-services.sh and scripts/dev-overnight-build-deploy.sh (neither exists on disk), but these appear in echo error messages as user guidance, not as direct invocations by the hook. Standard 10 does not cover hint strings in shell scripts. No violation under standard scope.
per_standard_notes.merge-conflicts: No unresolved merge conflict markers (<<<<<<, =======, >>>>>>>) found.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex identified three findings: (1) OBSERVATION_ONLY pre-existing: /tmp/claude-* paths at lines 155, 467, 1444 bypass CLAUDE_TMPDIR; (2) OBSERVATION_ONLY pre-existing: user-facing hint URLs at lines 1279, 1288 are not purely detection patterns and could be parameterized; (3) False-negative reasoning gap: Standard 10 verification was under-evidenced — scripts/deploy-services.sh and scripts/dev-overnight-build-deploy.sh referenced at lines 1225-1226 were not checked for existence. Codex confirmed no changed-line blocker in the current diff.

findings.issue: Standard 2/9: /tmp/claude-* paths at lines 155, 467, 1444 hardcode /tmp rather than deriving from CLAUDE_TMPDIR (line 9).
findings.proposed_fix: Derive from ${CLAUDE_TMPDIR%/} or add CLAUDE_ALLOWLIST_DIR variable. Apply to lines 155, 467, and 1444.
findings.rationale: Codex explicitly marks this OBSERVATION_ONLY and pre-existing outside the changed-files scope. The diff for this cycle modifies hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh but these /tmp paths are pre-existing code unchanged in this diff. Per --changed-files scoping rules this is out_of_scope for the current cycle's cleanliness gate.

findings.issue: Standard 2/9: User-facing environment hint strings at lines 1279 and 1288 (https://dev.life-ai.app, localhost:3005, api-dev.life-ai.app) are not purely security detection patterns — they are replacement endpoint suggestions that could be parameterized.
findings.proposed_fix: Define HAPPY_DEV_UI_URL=${HAPPY_DEV_UI_URL:-https://dev.life-ai.app} and HAPPY_DEV_API_HINT=${HAPPY_DEV_API_HINT:-localhost:3005 or api-dev.life-ai.app} and use those variables in echo strings.
findings.rationale: Codex explicitly marks this OBSERVATION_ONLY and pre-existing. These echo hint strings are fixed policy documentation for users, not runtime configuration. Pre-existing and outside the changed-files diff scope for this cycle.

findings.issue: Standard 10: Under-evidenced verification. scripts/deploy-services.sh and scripts/dev-overnight-build-deploy.sh referenced at lines 1225-1226 were not checked for existence in the original audit.
findings.proposed_fix: Either mark Standard 10 not applicable to .sh file script references, or enumerate all script-like references and verify each. Do not claim complete verification after checking only two library paths.
findings.rationale: Applied: checked both scripts via Glob. Neither scripts/deploy-services.sh nor scripts/dev-overnight-build-deploy.sh exists on disk. However, both appear exclusively in echo error message strings (user-facing hints), not as direct invocations by the hook. Standard 10 as written scopes to references in .md files and Python imports — not to hint strings in .sh files. No violation under the standard's defined scope. The per_standard_notes for dangling-references now documents this explicitly.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Finding 3 (under-evidenced Standard 10) was applied: verified the two referenced scripts do not exist, confirmed they appear only in user-facing hint echo strings (not direct invocations), and confirmed Standard 10 scope does not cover .sh hint strings. No change to violations list. Findings 1 and 2 are pre-existing OBSERVATION_ONLY items logged for future cycle consideration.
